notes: small photovoltaic panel, rated 9V/2W at full nominal sun (energy-sim.md §2.1) — a power SOURCE, not a passthrough like a regulator, but modeled here purely as a power_out pin with no power_in pin at all: it has no upstream electrical input to speak of (its 'input' is light), so it deliberately never becomes a passthrough candidate or a hot-graph seed in robosim's run mode. Whether it's actually producing anything (sun or no sun) is an energy-sim question, not a robowire static-legality one — statically, this is just a source rated for a worst-case output current, checked exactly like any other power_out pin (E02 voltage, E30 budget).
description: A small solar panel: converts light into DC power at a declared worst-case current, no batteries-included capacity concept the way a chemical cell has — just a rated output, same shape as any other regulated power source in this catalogue.
